t_ch: 我的考评绩效就靠您拯救了，{NICKNAME}……
t_en: My performance appraisal is all on you, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 怎么样，{NICKNAME}？您挑战过「星芒战幕」了吗？
t_en: What's up, {NICKNAME}? Have you had a go at Stellar Flare?

t_ch: 还没有…
t_en: Not yet...

t_ch: 这样啊…没关系，您慢慢来。
t_en: I see... Okay, take your time.

t_ch: 只要能赶上我交报告的死线…啊，不是，没什么，请您不要有压力……
t_en: So long as I can meet the deadline for my report... Ah, it's no big deal. Please don't feel pressured...

t_ch: 怎么样，{NICKNAME}？您挑战过「星芒战幕」了吗？
t_en: What's up, {NICKNAME}? Have you had a go at Stellar Flare?

t_ch: 打完一关了。
t_en: I finished one stage.

t_ch: 哇！真的吗，这么快？请…请问您体验如何？
t_en: Wow! Really, that fast? So... how was it?

t_ch: 轻轻松松。
t_en: Piece of cake.

t_ch: 果然，我就知道！像您这样的专家，通过这种程度的挑战肯定不在话下！
t_en: Aha, I knew it! Clearing a stage is a piece of cake for a pro like you!

t_ch: 有点难度。
t_en: Not really, no.

t_ch: 原来如此…居然能让您这样的专家都陷入苦战，这位设计师果然不是泛泛之辈呢。
t_en: So, that's how it is... The game designer must be quite talented if they can put even a pro like you through the wringer.

t_ch: 这个活动策划有点东西。
t_en: This event designer has a thing or two up their sleeve.

t_ch: 呃…您说的话，我好像不太能理解呢。
t_en: Ugh... I don't really understand what you're saying.

t_ch: 这样一来，我就能拿到测试数据了…今晚就开始写报告吧，我可不想让世玲姐反过来催我。
t_en: I can get my test data now... I'll start writing the report tonight. I don't want Miss Shiling rushing me about it.

t_ch: 太感谢您了，{NICKNAME}大救星！请一定拿着这些——这是我向科室申请的一些小小礼物，不成谢意！
t_en: Thank you so much, {NICKNAME} — you're a lifesaver! Please take these — just a few small gifts from our department. I owe you one!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您还在空间站呀？
t_en: Are you still in the space station, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 多亏了您的测试数据，我终于能向世玲姐交差了…您可能不了解，但这对我来说真的很重要…我可不希望试用期没过就被踢出应物科啊。
t_en: Thanks to the test data from you, I can finally submit my report to Miss Shiling... You might not realize this, but it's really important to me... I don't want to get kicked out of the Department of Implement Arts before my probation ends.

t_ch: 喔，对了，「星芒战幕」的关卡可不止一个，而且每种关卡都适配了三种难度——您如果感兴趣的话，不妨再多挑战几次。
t_en: Oh yeah, that's right — there's more than one stage in Stellar Flare, and each stage has three difficulty levels. If you want, you can keep playing it.

t_ch: 软件的安装包我已经发送到您的移动设备上了，或者您也可以用我旁边这台终端进行测试。希望您玩得开心……
t_en: I saved the installation package onto your mobile device — or you can just keep using this testing terminal next to me. Hope you have lots of fun playing...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您还在空间站呀？
t_en: Are you still in the space station, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 多亏了您的测试数据，我终于能向世玲姐交差了…您可能不了解，但这对我来说真的很重要…我可不希望试用期没过就被踢出应物科啊。
t_en: Thanks to the test data from you, I can finally submit my report to Miss Shiling... You might not realize this, but it's really important to me... I don't want to get kicked out of the Department of Implement Arts before my probation ends.

t_ch: 喔，对了，「星芒战幕」的关卡可不止一个，而且每种关卡都适配了三种难度——您如果感兴趣的话，不妨再多挑战几次。
t_en: Oh yeah, that's right — there's more than one stage in Stellar Flare, and each stage has three difficulty levels. If you want, you can keep playing it.

t_ch: 软件的安装包我已经发送到您的移动设备上了，或者您也可以用我旁边这台终端进行测试。希望您玩得开心……
t_en: I saved the installation package onto your mobile device — or you can just keep using this testing terminal next to me. Hope you have lots of fun playing...

t_ch: 我打通「超巨星」难度了。
t_en: I beat it on the Supergiant Star difficulty.

t_ch: …真的吗？可我连「矮星」难度都会卡关…人与人之间的差距果然比塔利亚星的大鸿沟还夸张……
t_en: ...Really? I can't even clear it on the Dwarf Star difficulty... I guess the differences in skill between people is even bigger than the drop of the Grand Chasm on the planet of Talia...

t_ch: 谢谢您，{NICKNAME}，这样我就又有新的数据可以写进报告里了。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. Now I've got even more data for my report.

t_ch: 我这儿还有些小东西…请您收下吧。
t_en: Here are some small rewards... Please, take it.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您还在空间站呀？
t_en: Are you still in the space station, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 多亏了您的测试数据，我终于能向世玲姐交差了…您可能不了解，但这对我来说真的很重要…我可不希望试用期没过就被踢出应物科啊。
t_en: Thanks to the test data from you, I can finally submit my report to Miss Shiling... You might not realize this, but it's really important to me... I don't want to get kicked out of the Department of Implement Arts before my probation ends.

t_ch: 喔，对了，「星芒战幕」的关卡可不止一个，而且每种关卡都适配了三种难度——您如果感兴趣的话，不妨再多挑战几次。
t_en: Oh yeah, that's right — there's more than one stage in Stellar Flare, and each stage has three difficulty levels. If you want, you can keep playing it.

t_ch: 软件的安装包我已经发送到您的移动设备上了，或者您也可以用我旁边这台终端进行测试。希望您玩得开心……
t_en: I saved the installation package onto your mobile device — or you can just keep using this testing terminal next to me. Hope you have lots of fun playing...

t_ch: 我通关「星芒战幕」了。
t_en: I clocked Stellar Flare.

t_ch: …通关？您的意思是…所有关卡、所有难度，全都通关了？
t_en: ...Clocked it? You mean... You finished all the stages on all difficulties?

t_ch: 这真是…太厉害了，{NICKNAME}！有了您收集的这些数据，我肯定能交出超出预期的测试报告——世玲姐一定会对我刮目相看的！
t_en: That's... utterly incredible, {NICKNAME}! With your data, I'll definitely be able to submit a report that exceeds expectations. This'll make Miss Shiling sit up and take notice!

t_ch: 我看看…喏，我这里还剩下些小东西…不成协议，请您一定收下！
t_en: Let me take a look... Here's a few small things... Just a small token of thanks. Go ahead, take it!

t_ch: 你不要太焦虑了。
t_en: Don't worry too much.

t_ch: 谢谢您的关心…空间站的传闻果然是真的，您是一位有胆识又会关心别人的大好人。
t_en: Thanks for caring... The rumor circulating in the space station is true! You're a brave and caring person.

t_ch: 能认识您真是太好了，我感觉工作又有动力了！
t_en: I'm so glad I get to meet you, I feel my passion for my work returning!

t_ch: 这、这怎么可能？明明两份密钥都没有被动过，她到底是怎么做到的……
t_en: H—How is this possible? Neither of the two encryption keys has been touched, so how on earth did she do it...

t_ch: 嗯？有什么事吗，找前台的话，在走廊另一侧…啊！怎么这边也报错了！？
t_en: Mmm? Are you encountering any problems? Just hit up the front desk down the corridor that way... Ah! What's this error doing here!?

t_ch: 你就是伦纳德？
t_en: Are you Leonard?

t_ch: 呃，是我…请问我们认识吗？
t_en: Mmm, that's me... Have we met?

t_ch: 我收到了和你有关的短信。
t_en: I got a text message that has something to do with you.

t_ch: 啊这…你也是垃圾短信的受害者？好吧，那确实和我有点关系……
t_en: Ah, that... Do you get spam messages too? Alright, I kinda have something to do with it...

t_ch: 我受到了和你有关的骚扰。
t_en: I got harassed because of you.

t_ch: 骚、骚扰？等一下，请听我解释！那条短信和我无关！不…也可能有那么点关系……
t_en: H—Harassed? Hang on, I can explain! That text message has nothing to do with me! No... I mean, it might have a little to do with me...

t_ch: 我想想，该从哪里说起呢？总之，先做个自我介绍…我叫伦纳德，是这座空间站的网络安全工程师。
t_en: Hmm, let me see. Where to begin? Anyway, let me introduce myself... My name's Leonard and I'm the cybersecurity engineer aboard this space station.

t_ch: 你应该知道吧？前不久，这里遭到了「反物质军团」的袭击。
t_en: You must've heard, right? The Antimatter Legion put out a hit on us not long ago.

t_ch: 知道，我就在现场。
t_en: I know. I was there.

t_ch: 哦，这样啊…看你的打扮，我还以为你不是空间站的人。
t_en: Oh, so... Judging by your style, I thought you were not from around here.

t_ch: 知道，我击退了好多虚卒。
t_en: I know. I fought off a bunch of Voidrangers.

t_ch: 那你可真厉害！我当时直奔疏散通道才捡回一条命。
t_en: Whoa, amazing! I bolted straight for the emergency chute to try and save my own neck.

t_ch: 知道，我就是军团的人。
t_en: I know. I'm one of the Legion.

t_ch: 呃…你是在开玩笑吧？这话可不兴说啊，要是被外人听见了，会把你报给「公司」的。
t_en: Uh... You're kidding, right? You can't just say that. If anyone hears, you'll be reported to IPC.

t_ch: 在那场骚乱中，各舱段的主控系统也因为爆炸产生的脉冲瘫痪了，我的工作就是协助修复这些设备，确认空间站的网络安全。
t_en: During the riot, the main control system for each module was paralyzed by the explosion. My job was to provide support for repairing these devices, and ensure network security of the space station.

t_ch: 修复工作还是很顺利的，只是过程中，我发现了一些异常——访问记录显示，在主控系统瘫痪的那段时间，有「军团」以外的第三者入侵了这里。
t_en: The repair job began smoothly, but along the way I found some anomalies — some access logs showing that a third party other than the Legion was actually here, while the systems were all paralyzed.

t_ch: 是个戴墨镜的女人？
t_en: Was it a lady in sunglasses?

t_ch: 呃，抱歉，我不清楚对方的长相，但入侵者应该不止一人。
t_en: Mmm, sorry but I don't know what they look like — only that there should be more than one of them.

t_ch: 是星核猎手？
t_en: Stellaron Hunters?

t_ch: 嗯，有人看法和你一致。
t_en: Mmm, some others have surmised the same as you.

t_ch: 是星穹列车？
t_en: The Astral Express?

t_ch: 不是，他们到的要更早些。
t_en: No, they were quite a while earlier.

t_ch: 这和短信有什么关系？
t_en: What's that got to do with text messages?

t_ch: 呃，马上就到重点了。
t_en: Oh, I'll get to the point shortly.

t_ch: 虽然没有证据，但我们怀疑来者是「星核猎手」。这群人神出鬼没，危险得很，个个都在「公司」通缉中榜上有名。
t_en: I have no evidence for this, but we suspect the intruders were Stellaron Hunters. These guys are elusive and highly dangerous. Every single one is on IPC's wanted list.

t_ch: 其中还有个超级黑客，我看过她的资料，竟然能和天才俱乐部的成员打个平手，厉害啊…如果真的是她，能轻松绕过空间站的监控也不奇怪。
t_en: One of them is a genius hacker. I've read her profile, and she's right up there with the members of the Genius Society. Just incredible... If it really is her, then it's no surprise that the space station's surveillance systems were bypassed.

t_ch: 我本来已经做好了没法交差的准备，可没想到，在筛查过程中，我竟然发现了一些入侵者留下的蛛丝马迹。
t_en: I was planning on submitting my helpless report, but never expected to find some clues left behind by the intruders during the investigation.

t_ch: 虽然直觉告诉我好像有哪里不对劲，但这可是和星核猎手交锋的机会诶…于是我鼓起勇气，沿着这条线索追踪了下去……
t_en: Even though my intuition tells me something is fishy, I couldn't pass up an opportunity to lock horns with the Stellaron Hunters... So I plucked up my courage and kept pulling on this clue I'd found...

t_ch: 然后呢？
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 如你所见…我中了对方的陷阱！
t_en: And as you can see... I fell right into their trap!

t_ch: 感觉猜到了结局……
t_en: I can hazard a guess about how things turned out...

t_ch: 嗯，如你所见…我中了对方的陷阱！
t_en: Mmm, as you can see... I fell right into their trap!

t_ch: 是陷阱吧？
t_en: Is it a trap?

t_ch: 没错。如你所见…我中了对方的陷阱！
t_en: Yes, as you can see... I fell right into their trap!

t_ch: 是我太天真了，那可是星核猎手啊…在信息攻防中，我的设备被对方反向植入了一种病毒，开始不受控制地向外发送信息。
t_en: I'm too naive, and it was a Stellaron Hunter... During the cyber-attack, a virus was uploaded to my device, and it started helplessly spamming out messages.

t_ch: 是的，就是你收到的那条短信。
t_en: Yeah, that's the text message you got.

t_ch: 这就是垃圾短信的始末了。现在我的名字传遍了整个空间站，人人都知道有个叫伦纳德的呆瓜闯了大祸，职业生涯已经彻底完蛋了吧，哈哈……
t_en: That's the whole story about those spam messages. Now I'm infamous throughout the whole space station — everyone knows about the guy called Leonard who stuffed up big time. My engineering career is over. Ahaha... *sigh*

t_ch: 不是你的错。
t_en: It's not your fault.

t_ch: 谢谢…希望艾丝妲站长也这么想。
t_en: Thank you... Hope the Lead Researcher Asta may say the same.

t_ch: 是对手太强了。
t_en: They're just too good.

t_ch: 谢谢，我对此深有体会。
t_en: Thank you, I realize that now.

t_ch: 彻底完蛋了，哈哈。
t_en: Completely over, haha.

t_ch: 哈哈！
t_en: Haha!

t_ch: 至于短信中提到的《太空大战争》是什么…这个解释起来比较复杂，你还是直接看一眼实物吧。
t_en: As for the Cosmic Warfare mentioned in the message... That's harder to explain, so it's better if you see it with your own eyes.

t_ch: 就是我手边这台电脑，你调查一下就明白了。
t_en: This computer I have here. Take a look at it and you'll see what I mean.

t_ch: 监控室中央放着一台平平无奇的电脑……
t_en: There's an ordinary computer terminal in the middle of the monitoring room...

t_ch: （按下回车键。）
t_en: (Press Enter.)

t_ch: （按下后退键。）
t_en: (Press Backspace.)

t_ch: （按下重启键。）
t_en: (Press Reset.)

t_ch: 「哟，你好啊！屏幕那边正在焦头烂额的朋友。」
t_en: "Hello there, distressed friend at the computer terminal."

t_ch: 这是…？
t_en: What's this...?

t_ch: 一段预设好的留言，正在通过监控室的屏幕播放。
t_en: A pre-recorded message blares out of the screen in the monitoring room.

t_ch: 呃，你好？
t_en: Uh... hello?

t_ch: 你打招呼她也听不见的…这只是一段预设好的留言，正在通过监控室的屏幕播放。
t_en: She can't hear anything you say... This is a pre-recorded message blaring out of the screen in the monitoring room.

t_ch: 有入侵者！快跑！
t_en: Intruder alert! Evacuate!

t_ch: 别紧张…这只是一段预设好的留言，正在通过监控室的屏幕播放。
t_en: Don't worry... This is a pre-recorded message blaring out of the screen in the monitoring room.

t_ch: 你也可以把它想成一封挑战书，或是胜者的嘲笑…那人反向骇入了我的设备，占据了这里。
t_en: You can also think of it as a written challenge, or a taunt from the winner... From the guy who hacked into my device and took over the whole place.

t_ch: 「首先要夸夸你，做得不错。没几个人能找到这个漏洞，连我自己都没发现。」
t_en: "First of all, I have to praise you on a job well done. Not many know about this exploit, not even myself."

t_ch: 「你在追查我的事，对不？那咱们来比一场吧。」
t_en: "You're trying to catch me, aren't you? Then how about a little contest?"

t_ch: 「我准备了一个游戏，只要你能通关，我们就可以聊聊——假如你是个高手，咱们还能交个朋友。」
t_en: "I built a game. If you can clear it, then we can chat. If you're a gaming master, we might even be able to become friends."

t_ch: 「但要是你输了…倒也不会有什么损失，可你之后想要再找到我，那就难咯。」
t_en: "But if you lose... Well, you won't lose anything, but you'll find it very difficult to find me later."

t_ch: 「另外，这个游戏需要多人协作。为了帮你找些场外援助，我以你的名义给全空间站的人发送了邀请函，试着和大家一起合力攻克难关吧。」
t_en: "Also, this is a multiplayer game. So to give you some help, I used your account to send out an invitation to everyone aboard the space station. Try and see if you can conquer this challenge together with everyone's help."

t_ch: 「那么加油，祝你好运。」
t_en: "Good luck. I'm rooting for you."

t_ch: 如何，你能理解现状了吧？
t_en: What do you think? Do you understand what's going on now?

t_ch: 轻轻松松。
t_en: Piece of cake.

t_ch: 嗯。毕竟以她的说法，这应该只能算是新手教学。
t_en: Um. After all, as she'd put it: This is like a tutorial.

t_ch: 难如登天。
t_en: Hard as diamonds.

t_ch: 呃…这样吗？没事，按她的说法，这是个多人协作游戏。大家一起努力，总归有办法的……
t_en: Uh... really? That's okay. According to her, it's a multiplayer game. With everyone working together, we'll find a way...

t_ch: 像是在玩游戏。
t_en: It's like playing a game.

t_ch: 准确地说，我们就是在玩游戏…虽然不知道对方的真正用意是什么。
t_en: To be precise, we are the ones playing a game... Even though we don't know what the creator's intentions are.

t_ch: 就像你看到的…这是挑衅，她整了个面向全空间站的游戏，还把监控室变成了街机厅。
t_en: It's just as you've seen... One massive taunt. She's developed a game for the entire space station, turning the monitoring room into an arcade.

t_ch: 目的是什么？拖延时间…还是分散注意力？我不知道，黑客的心思谁能理解呢……
t_en: What's her purpose? Buying more time... Or a misdirection? I don't know, who even understands the motivations of a hacker...

t_ch: 总之，这段时间我会一直待在这儿，试着解决问题的。你如果有兴趣，也可以照她说的试试，说不定真能通关呢…啊！怎么又报错了！？
t_en: Anyway, I'll just be cooped up in here trying to fix this problem. Give it a go just like she said if you're interested. Maybe you can beat the game? Gawd, another error!?

t_ch: 好忙啊…好忙啊…好忙啊……
t_en: So busy... So busy... So busy...

t_ch: 我想问你点事…
t_en: There is something I want to ask you...

t_ch: 怎么了？你说吧。
t_en: What's up? Speak.

t_ch: 关于你…
t_en: About you...

t_ch: 我吗？
t_en: Me?

t_ch: 平时很少遇见你。
t_en: I rarely bump into you.

t_ch: 哦，毕竟我的工作性质比较特殊嘛，不是在机房做维护，就是在谁的办公室检查电脑，偶尔也会离开舱段干点室外活儿。
t_en: Oh, that's the special nature of my work. If I'm not maintaining machines in the terminal room, I'll be in someone's office checking up on their computer. Sometimes I have to leave the zone for other jobs, too.

t_ch: 没办法，维修科人力不足，招也招不到…你平时见不到我是很正常的。
t_en: It's just how it is. The maintenance team is understaffed. They can't recruit anyone... So that's why you hardly see me around.

t_ch: 你以前是做什么的？
t_en: What was your job before?

t_ch: 以前？我一直是工程师，之前为「星际和平公司」工作，定级是…P<unbreak>24</unbreak>？P<unbreak>25</unbreak>？记不清了，我离开那里有好一阵子了。
t_en: Before this? I've always been an engineer. I used to work for the Interastral Peace Corporation, and I made rank... P<unbreak>24</unbreak>? P<unbreak>25</unbreak>, I think? I can't remember, for it's been a while since I worked there.

t_ch: 公司是个好地方，就是太大了，我这种分部的小职员，可能忙活半辈子也没法和那些大咖见上一面。
t_en: I liked that workplace, but it was too big. A small fry like me could spend half a lifetime there without bumping into any bigwigs...

t_ch: 空间站就不一样了，抬头低头都能见着艾丝妲站长。要是运气好，你还能被黑塔女士骂上几句——那可是天才俱乐部的黑塔诶！
t_en: The space station is different — you can hardly escape the Lead Researcher Asta. If you're lucky, you can even get your ears chewed off by Madam Herta — the same Herta from the Genius Society!

t_ch: 我想问别的…
t_en: I want to ask about something else...

t_ch: 关于这起事件…
t_en: About this incident...

t_ch: 嗯，你说吧。
t_en: Mhm, go ahead.

t_ch: 为什么是《太空大战争》？
t_en: Why's it called Cosmic Warfare?

t_ch: 《太空大战争》吗？嗯…说实话，我也不知道。我没听说过这游戏，问了下熟悉这方面的朋友，好像是部有些年头的作品。
t_en: Cosmic Warfare? To be honest... I dunno. I've never heard of this game. I asked all my buddies who know about this kind of thing, and I think it's some kind of an old game.

t_ch: 这游戏一般是搭载在那种老式星际驳船的破显示器上的。呃…感觉只有闲得要命的醉酒矿工才会玩。
t_en: It's from the old display screens on those antique interstellar skiffs. Ugh... The type of game only an idle drunk miner could love.

t_ch: 而且原版的《太空大战争》怎么可能会有反物质军团啊，她应该是在游戏程序上动了些手脚，至于目的…我就不知道了
t_en: And the original version of Cosmic Warfare never featured the Antimatter Legion, anyway. She's just played around with the game's engine. As for her aim... I have no idea.

t_ch: 你对这个黑客了解多少？
t_en: How much do you know about this hacker?

t_ch: 仅限于看过档案的程度，不过她之前生活的地方我倒是很熟悉。「朋克洛德」，是个很开放的赛博星球。
t_en: Only what I could read from the program file. I do know a bit about where she's from: Punklorde — an extremely open cyberworld.

t_ch: 那里盛产黑客，还有一帮人把现实当作大型沉浸式游戏，听着就是个人才济济的风水宝地，出个星核猎手不奇怪。
t_en: They're famous for their hackers, and some even regarded reality as a kind of immersive MMORPG. It's a world of promised land stacked with outstanding talent, so I'm not surprised they've produced a Stellaron Hunter there...

t_ch: 你这边进展如何？
t_en: How have you been getting on with things?

t_ch: 不怎么样。我尽力把病毒程序的影响范围控制在了监控室内，至少不用担心空间站出什么乱子，但更多的…还有待努力。
t_en: Not the best... I've done my best to isolate the intruder's viral influence to the monitoring room. I don't have to worry about other troubles throughout the space station, but as for everything else... It all needs more work.

t_ch: 维修科的老大说这样就行，现在空间站要解决的麻烦太多了，既然对方没有进一步攻击的打算，我们就先稳住阵地。
t_en: My supervisor at the Maintenance Department said this is all fine. As of now, the space station has too much on its plate to handle. Since they're not making any further attacks, I should just keep it stabilized here.

t_ch: 我想问别的…
t_en: I want to ask about something else...

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 哦，我还写了个脚本，可以没日没夜地拦截那些垃圾短信。虽然办法原始了点，但多少也能发挥点作用。
t_en: Also, I wrote a script to block off those spam messages all day, every day... It's a little primitive, but it'll still be useful to some extent.

t_ch: 不，没什么。
t_en: Nothing.

t_ch: 好的，如果有需要，随时来找我。
t_en: Okay. Come find me anytime you need something.

t_ch: 「哟，你好啊，屏幕那边正在焦头烂额的朋友！」
t_en: "Hello there, distressed friend at the computer terminal!"

t_ch: 启动「太空大战争」。
t_en: Launch Cosmic Warfare.

t_ch: 「加油，祝你好运。」
t_en: "Good luck, I'm rooting for you."

t_ch: 我想问你点事…
t_en: There is something I want to ask you...

t_ch: 「虽然不知道你的名字，但还是要夸夸你，做得真不错！没几个人能发现我留下的这个漏洞。」
t_en: "I don't even know your name, but I want to praise you on a job well done! Not many people at all know about this exploit."

t_ch: 「你在追查我的事，对不？那咱们来比一场吧……」
t_en: "You're trying to catch me, aren't you? Then how about a little contest..."

t_ch: 屏幕上重复着机械的对话，这只是一段预设好的留言。看来想知道些什么，必须得通关这个游戏。
t_en: The same message repeats itself on the screen — it's just a pre-recorded message. Looks like if you want to get more information, you'll have to first win the game.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 「嗯，再见啦。」
t_en: "Okay, seeya later."

t_ch: 你知道监控室变成了街机厅吗…
t_en: Did you know the monitoring room has become an arcade...

t_ch: 嘘，这件事我已经知道啦，伦纳德发出的第一条短信就是给我的。
t_en: Shh— I already know about that. The first person Leonard messaged was me.

t_ch: 但现在空间站还在恢复期，我不想与此无关的大家有额外的烦心事，所以先别让这件事继续扩散啦，帮我保个密，好吗？
t_en: But now that the space station is under maintenance, I don't want to add any undue stress to staff who aren't involved. So let's not spread this news yet, okay? Let's keep things hush-hush, alright?

t_ch: 好。
t_en: Alright.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}，我知道你会理解我~
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. I knew you'd understand!

t_ch: 不好。
t_en: It's not alright.

t_ch: 唔，好吧，{NICKNAME}是个有个性的人呢…没关系，我会和所有知道了这件事的人再单独谈谈的。
t_en: Mmm, alright, {NICKNAME} is an adamant person... That's okay. I'll just talk to everyone who knows about this one-on-one.

t_ch: 至于伦纳德啊…请相信他吧。自从空间站被入侵后，他就一直待在机房里工作，几乎没怎么休息过，卡里维尔家族的人都是这个性格。
t_en: As for Leonard... Just trust him. Since the breach, he's been living in his terminal room and was hardly taking breaks. The Calliwells are all like that.

t_ch: 我相信他能妥善处理这件事，维修科也在密切关注，如果还是不放心吧，你就多帮帮他吧。
t_en: I trust he can handle this properly. The Maintenance Department is keeping a close eye on this one. If you're still worried about him, then give him more support.

t_ch: 你知道监控室变成了街机厅吗…
t_en: Did you know the monitoring room has become an arcade...

t_ch: 街机厅？不如模拟宇宙。
t_en: Arcade? Simulated Universes are way more fun.

t_ch: 你不好奇发生了什么？
t_en: Aren't you curious about what happened?

t_ch: 能有什么，猜也知道又有人闯祸了呗，这事难道很稀奇吗？
t_en: What can it be? I can think with my knees and still know that someone messed up. Is that really a rare occurrence?

t_ch: 这事和星核猎手有关。
t_en: This has something to do with the Stellaron Hunters.

t_ch: 哦，你要说这个，我还有点兴趣。
t_en: Oh, those guys. You've piqued my interest.

t_ch: 我一点也不奇怪，人家好歹是能和螺丝咕姆的差分机球打个平手的黑客，骇入这个纸糊的空间站还不是轻而易举？
t_en: I'm not surprised at all. They're hackers on a level with Screwllum. This space station's security must be like a kindergarten to them.

t_ch: 她能入侵第一次，自然就能做到第二次，以后说不定还有第三次。
t_en: If she can invade the first time around, then she can do it a second time — or even a third time.

t_ch: 星核猎手的事我自有打算，你就别操心了。需要你知道的时候，我自然会告诉你。
t_en: I have my own plans for the Stellaron Hunters, so don't worry. When I need you to know, you'll know.

t_ch: 必须教育一下伦纳德。
t_en: Leonard must be taught a lesson.

t_ch: 谁啊？
t_en: Who?

t_ch: 反正，如果你想解决问题，就去找艾丝妲；如果你想解决人，那也找艾丝妲。
t_en: Anyway, if you want to fix a problem, find Asta. If you want to fix a person, find Asta, too.

t_ch: 我和艾丝妲聊过了。
t_en: I chatted with Asta.

t_ch: 什、什么？ 你不会指的是这里的事吧……
t_en: Wh—What? You can't mean what's going on here, can you...

t_ch: 那…艾丝妲站长是怎么说的？
t_en: Well... What did the Lead Researcher Asta say?

t_ch: 艾丝妲相信你可以解决危机。
t_en: Asta believes you can fix everything.

t_ch: 啊，站长真是这么说的？太好了，看来我不用引咎辞职了…谢谢你告诉我这些，我一定不会辜负站长的期待！
t_en: Ah, she really said that? That's great... Looks like I won't have to resign, after all. Thanks for letting me know about all this. I won't let down our lead researcher!

t_ch: 艾丝妲让我来帮你。
t_en: Asta asked me to help you.

t_ch: 这、这样吗？看来站长还是很担心啊，但至少我不用引咎辞职了吧…应该？总之，我们一起加油！
t_en: R—Really? Looks like the lead researcher is still really concerned, but at least I don't have to fall on my sword and resign... Right? Anyway, let's do this!

t_ch: 艾丝妲很生气。
t_en: Asta's really angry.

t_ch: 啊？这、这…唔，也对，是我辜负了她的信任。如果当初用的是分割加密，空间站也许就不会被入侵了吧……
t_en: Ah? Th—This... Oh, I've betrayed her trust. If I'd used partition encryption right from the start, we'd never have been hacked...

t_ch: 但现在后悔也无济于事…唉，还是先继续解决问题吧……
t_en: But it's no use regretting things now... Ack, let's get back to solving the problem at hand.

t_ch: 我和黑塔聊过了。
t_en: I've chatted with Herta.

t_ch: 什——什么！？你不会指的是这里的事吧！
t_en: Wh—What? You can't mean what's going on here, can you...!

t_ch: 黑塔女士也知道了？完了，这下全完了……
t_en: Herta knows, too? This time it's game over...

t_ch: 她说她不关心。
t_en: She said she didn't care.

t_ch: 是啊，言外之意不就是让我自觉滚蛋吗？全完了全完了…这下得罪了最不该最得罪的人，我别想在这里继续待下去了啊……
t_en: Yup, isn't the implication for me to beat it? It's over, it's all over... I've offended the last person that should ever be offended. There is no way I could ever keep on staying here like this...

t_ch: 她说她很关心这事！
t_en: She said she cared about this!

t_ch: 感觉更可怕了啊！全完了全完了…这下得罪了最不该最得罪的人，我别想在这里继续待下去了啊……
t_en: That's even worse! It's over, it's all over... I've offended the last person that should ever be offended. There is no way I could ever keep on staying here like this...

t_ch: 等处理完这件事，我就递交辞呈，卷铺盖跑路吧…只是现在，还不能放着这里的烂摊子不管……
t_en: Once I've dealt with this mess, I can submit my resignation, curl up, and vanish... But right now, I can't just leave this mess like this...

t_ch: 监控室中央放着一台平平无奇的电脑……
t_en: There's an ordinary computer terminal in the middle of the monitoring room...

t_ch: （按下回车键。）
t_en: (Press Enter.)

t_ch: 什么也没有发生，这只是一台平平无奇的电脑。
t_en: Nothing happened. It's just a normal computer terminal.

t_ch: （按下后退键。）
t_en: (Press Backspace.)

t_ch: 什么也没有发生，这只是一台平平无奇的电脑。
t_en: Nothing happened. It's just a normal computer terminal.

t_ch: （按下重启键。）
t_en: (Press Reset.)

t_ch: 它重启了，这只是一台平平无奇的电脑。
t_en: It reboots. It's just a normal computer terminal.

t_ch: 喂，你在干什么！？
t_en: Hey, what are you doing!?

t_ch: 呼，你来啦？看，病毒程序消失了，事情总算告一段落了。
t_en: Oh, you're here? Look, the virus has been cleared and it seems like things are all over for now.

t_ch: 恭喜啊。
t_en: Congratulations.

t_ch: 好歹是平安解决了。这样一来，我也算是和天才黑客交过手的人了……
t_en: Anyway, everything turned out alright. In this regard, I guess I'm someone who's taken on a genius hacker, right...

t_ch: 我们两个太强啦。
t_en: We're formidable together.

t_ch: 好歹是平安解决了。这样一来，我们也算是和天才黑客交过手的人了……
t_en: Anyway, everything turned out alright. In this regard, I guess we're people who've taken on a genius hacker, right...

t_ch: 你也没做什么吧。
t_en: You didn't do anything.

t_ch: 别这么说啊！至少空间站其他地方一点都没受到影响——我没有功劳也有苦劳吧。
t_en: Don't say that! At least the rest of the space station wasn't impacted — I at least deserve credit for back-breaking labor.

t_ch: 不过我还是觉得很奇怪，这家伙这么大费周章，真的只是为了找一堆人陪她玩游戏？完全没有别的目的？
t_en: But I still think it's strange how this hacker goes to all this trouble, just to play games with a bunch of people. Does she really have no other goal here?

t_ch: 唉，总感觉此事还有蹊跷，但我得先思考事故报告该怎么写了…希望不会演变成辞呈吧……
t_en: Alas, I always felt something was off, but I have to turn my attention to writing up a situation report... Hope it doesn't turn into a resignation letter...

t_ch: 「哟，做得不错啊。本来我不抱希望的，没想到空间站还真有人能通关这个游戏。」
t_en: "Yo, great job. I wasn't holding my breath and didn't expect anyone from the space station could clear this game."

t_ch: 对话不一样了…
t_en: Now the words are different...

t_ch: 「因为这不是留言，是我本人在和你对话。」
t_en: "Because this isn't pre-recorded. It's me, talking right now with you."

t_ch: 你是本人？
t_en: It's "you"?

t_ch: 「对，正在屏幕那边和你实时对话。」
t_en: "Yep, I'm talking with you on-screen in real-time."

t_ch: 「好吧，依照约定，咱们可以谈一谈了，你有什么想聊的？」
t_en: "According to the agreement, we're allowed to talk. Anything you want to chat about?"

t_ch: 为什么是《太空大战争》？
t_en: Why's it called Cosmic Warfare?

t_ch: 「哦，这事没那么复杂。有个星际收藏家在征集这游戏的彩蛋攻略，最高奖励是一架泰伦战机，正是我想要的。」
t_en: "Oh, there's not much to it. There's a collector looking for a rare strategy for this game, and the top prize is a Tyranid fighter jet, which is exactly what I want."

t_ch: 「但《太空大战争》是个多人协作游戏，只靠我一人玩不下去，还得有朋友才行，越多越好。我正发愁呢，没想到有人自己送上了门。」
t_en: "But Cosmic Warfare is a multiplayer co-op game, so I can't play it alone. I need friends to play it — the more the merrier. I was just worrying about this, and never expected the answer to fall right into my lap."

t_ch: 那些垃圾短信是…
t_en: What are those spam messages all about?

t_ch: 「我自己写的，为了营造氛围，参考了公司的广告邮件，学得挺像的，对不对？…等一下，你刚才是不是说了『垃圾』？」
t_en: "I wrote those based on the IPC's advertising mails to build up ambiance. Don't you think they sound similar? ...Hang on, did you just say spam?"

t_ch: 有更实在点的奖励吗…
t_en: Is there a more practical reward available...

t_ch: 「那你把得分的屏幕拍张照片吧，然后想办法寄到朋克洛德，如果运气好，没准能拿到一个刺绣徽章，那儿的人都爱这套。」
t_en: "Snap a screenshot, and find a way to send it to Punklorde. If you're lucky, you might get an embroidered badge. They love that kind of stuff."

t_ch: 我想问你的事…
t_en: I'd like to ask you about...

t_ch: 「哦，可以啊，你随便问。」
t_en: "Oh yes, ask whatever you like."

t_ch: 我认识你。
t_en: I know you.

t_ch: 「哈哈，你还记得啊。我也没想到会以这种方式再见到你，咱们还挺有缘的。」
t_en: "Haha, you remember me? I never thought we'd be meeting like this again, either. Call it fate."

t_ch: 你的目的是什么？
t_en: What is your objective?

t_ch: 「我说了，只是玩个游戏而已，这事很不可思议吗，非得有个目的才行？」
t_en: "I said, it's just a game. Isn't it incredible? Do I have to have an ulterior motive?"

t_ch: 「哦，如果你指的是星核猎手…那抱歉，问错人啦。我只是奉命行事，你应该去问卡芙卡——你的事，她向来比我更关心。」
t_en: "Oh, if you mean the Stellaron Hunters... Then sorry, I'm the wrong person to ask. I'm just following orders. You should ask Kafka — She's always been more interested in your stuff than me."

t_ch: 为什么会加入星核猎手？
t_en: Why did you join the Stellaron Hunters?

t_ch: 「怪问题。你会一直待在新手村吗？朋克洛德是个人人都自以为能掌控现实的无聊地方，盛产没有保修的义肢、伪造的通行证，还有天才黑客——」
t_en: "Weird question but okay. Do you always just stay in the beginner's zone, Punklorde? Punklorde is a boring place where everyone thinks they're the master of reality. A place that's full of knock-off prosthetics, fake passes Accounts, and also some genius hackers..."

t_ch: 「而其中最强的一位，正在和你对话。」
t_en: "The most skilled of which, is currently talking to you."

t_ch: 我想问点别的…
t_en: I want to ask about something else...

t_ch: 没什么想聊的了。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 「好啦，谢谢你，我终于攻略了《太空大战争》——和陌生人一起游戏的感觉还不坏，对吧？」
t_en: "Alright, thanks. I've finally mastered Cosmic Warfare — It's not too bad playing online with strangers, right?"

t_ch: 「这之后的事，你应该能猜到了。放心，我早说过，只是玩个游戏而已。」
t_en: "You oughta be able to guess what happens next. Don't worry. I told you it's just a game, nothing more."

t_ch: 「你的游戏水平还不赖，我记住了，期待以后能和你再玩上几局。」
t_en: "Your gaming skills aren't too bad at all, if I recall correctly. Looking forward to playing a few more rounds with you later."

t_ch: 「我想离开那里，而星核猎手也欢迎我加入——就这么简单。」
t_en: "I wanted out of there, and the Stellaron Hunters were very welcoming — it's that simple."

t_ch: 「我已经停止了攻击，病毒应该很快就会被空间站的人清除了。在那之前，你还有一小段时间可以尽情享受这个游戏。」
t_en: "I've stopped the attack, and the virus should be cleared by techs on the space station soon. Until then, you still have a short while in which you can keep playing the game."

t_ch: 「祝你玩得开心，我先走一步啦。」
t_en: "Have fun. I'll see myself out."

t_ch: 「哟，你好啊，屏幕那边正在焦头烂额的朋友！」
t_en: "Hello there, distressed friend at the computer terminal!"

t_ch: 启动「太空大战争」。
t_en: Launch Cosmic Warfare.

t_ch: 「加油，祝你好运。」
t_en: "Good luck, I'm rooting for you."

t_ch: 变回留言了…
t_en: Back to the messages...

t_ch: 「虽然不知道你的名字，但还是要夸夸你，做得真不错！没几个人能发现我留下的这个漏洞。」
t_en: "I don't even know your name, but I want to praise you on a job well done! Not many people at all know about this exploit."

t_ch: 「你在追查我的事，对不？那咱们来比一场吧。」
t_en: "You're trying to catch me, aren't you? Then how about a little contest?"

t_ch: 屏幕上重复着机械的对话，这只是一段预设好的留言。看来屏幕另一边的人已经离开了。
t_en: The same message repeats itself on the screen — it's just a pre-recorded message. Looks like the person on the other end has already left.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 「嗯，再见啦。」
t_en: "Okay, seeya later."

t_ch: 我来帮忙收集展品了。
t_en: I'm here to help collect the exhibits.

t_ch: 喔？你来了啊。这次是准备收集<color=#dbc291ff>「贝洛伯格风景画」</color>，对吗？
t_en: Oh? Hi there. You're here to collect <color=#dbc291ff>Belobog Landscape Paintings</color>, right?

t_ch: 如你所见，综合馆里现在还缺三幅同类展品——这些画作都出自大师<color=#dbc291ff>雷比尼</color>之手……
t_en: As you can see, three exhibits of the same category are still missing in the General Hall. They are all made by the great painter <color=#dbc291ff>Rebini</color>.

t_ch: 他出身于贝洛伯格艺术学院，是当今「巡回艺术画派」的代表人物，可以说，当今贝城画坛巨擘非他莫属了。
t_en: A graduate of the Belobog College of Art, he is currently the icon of the nomadist school and the pinnacle of this city's art scene.

t_ch: 虽然他之前承诺了会按时交稿，但我也清楚这人的性子…想必他是又把工作全都攒到最后，现正不知道在哪儿疯狂赶稿呢。
t_en: Although he has promised to submit his commissions on time, I know him too well... He must have procrastinated till the last minute. I bet he is working flat out somewhere right now to complete his work.

t_ch: 所以我要去他工作室催稿？
t_en: So I gotta pay him a visit in his studio to chase him up?

t_ch: 不，你去工作室肯定找不着他。他平生最痛恨的就是闭门造车——但凡有机会，他是一定会出门写生的。
t_en: No, I'm sure he won't be there. He hates doing his work in isolation. If possible, he will definitely paint outdoors.

t_ch: 不知道在哪儿？他畏罪潜逃了？
t_en: No clue where he's at? Did he just bail out on you to avoid trouble?

t_ch: 哈哈，那倒不至于。雷比尼他平生最痛恨的就是闭门造车——但凡有机会，他是一定会出门写生的。
t_en: Haha. Unlikely. He hates doing his work in isolation. If possible, he will definitely paint outdoors.

t_ch: 让我看看，现在还缺哪些画……
t_en: Let me check which paintings are still missing...

t_ch: …好了，我知道了。看来，得麻烦你去趟<color=#dbc291ff>城郊雪原</color>了——不出意外的话，他人应该就在那里。
t_en: ...Got it. Looks like I'll have to task you with a trip to the <color=#dbc291ff>Outlying Snow Plains</color>. If everything goes as planned, he should be over there.

t_ch: 我去去就回。
t_en: I'm on it and I'll be back in a jiffy.

t_ch: 好，那就拜托你了……
t_en: Great, off you go then...

t_ch: 要是出意外了呢？
t_en: What if things don't go as planned?

t_ch: 到时候我会亲自去工作室找他。要是那里也没人，我们就只能祈祷他平安无事了……
t_en: Then I'll go visit his studio myself. If nobody's there, all we can do is pray that he's safe and well...

t_ch: …啊，对了，你把这个拿上：我们之前约定稿费现结，这是货款。他要是画完了，你就直接把钱交给他吧。
t_en: ...Oh yeah, take this. We've agreed to settle things in cash, and here's the money. If he's done painting, then you can hand this over to him immediately.

t_ch: 不过，这小子脾气比较古怪。如果他坚持不要稿费，你也就别浪费时间了——就当是跑腿费，自己收下吧。
t_en: But this dude's got a quirky temper. If he insists on not taking the payment, don't waste your time. Just keep it as a delivery fee for yourself.

t_ch: 林中雪地的寂静中，回响着你脚步的乐声——
t_en: The melody of your footsteps echoes in the silent snowdrift...

t_ch: 就像缓缓飘移的幽灵——你来到冬日的严寒中——！
t_en: Just like a drifting snow phantom — you've come in the dead of winter!

t_ch: 所以——你是谁——？
t_en: And so, you are...?

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}！多么悦耳的名字！幸会幸会！
t_en: {NICKNAME}! What a delightful name! Nice to meet you.

t_ch: 咦，我们是不是之前见过……
t_en: Hmm, have we met somewhere before...

t_ch: …算了，那不重要！鄙人名叫雷比尼，现在正为贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆贡献展品锐意写生中！
t_en: ...Ah, forget it. That's not important! My name's Rebini, and I'm currently absorbed in creating an exhibit for the Belobog History and Culture Museum!

t_ch: 所以，{NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#女士}，您找鄙人有何贵干？
t_en: So then, {M#Sir}{F#Ma'am} {NICKNAME}, what can I humbly do for you?

t_ch: 来和您聊聊艺术。
t_en: I've come to talk art with you.

t_ch: 哎呀，这可不得了…竟然有人特地来这冰天雪地里找鄙人交流艺术……
t_en: Heavens, that's unbelievable... Someone is coming all the way out to this icy frostscape just to talk art with me...

t_ch: 正好正好，鄙人刚完成了这幅风景画——贝洛伯格的远景——我准备给它起名为《晨曦》！
t_en: What great timing. I've just finished with this landscape piece — a vista overlooking Belobog — and I'm gonna call it Ray of Dawn!

t_ch: 不过，画是画完了…鄙人总觉得哪里好像欠了些什么。既然您也是懂艺术的，还烦请您给掌掌眼——您觉得，这画作究竟如何？
t_en: It's finished, but... I can't help but feel like it's missing something, somewhere. Seeing as you're someone who understands art, could you give this a once over? What do you think of this painting?

t_ch: 于尔丹托我带个话…
t_en: Uldan sent me with a message...

t_ch: 停！打住！怎么又是那个晦气的老头子？都说了快了快了马上画完，还催！
t_en: Stop! Stop that! That wretched old man again!? I told him, soon, soon — I'll finish it soon! Quit bugging me!

t_ch: 不过，你倒是来得正好。鄙人刚完成了这幅风景画——贝洛伯格的远景——我准备给它起名为《晨曦》！
t_en: But you've got great timing. I've just finished with this landscape — a vista overlooking Belobog — and I'm gonna call it Ray of Dawn!

t_ch: 但画是画完了…鄙人总觉得哪里好像欠了些什么——在你看来，这画作究竟如何？
t_en: It's finished, but... I can't help but feel like it's missing something, somewhere. What do you think of this painting?

t_ch: 我是替于尔丹来催稿的。
t_en: Uldan sent me to chase up this painting.

t_ch: 于尔丹！多么悦耳的名字！幸会幸会……
t_en: Uldan! What a delightful name! Nice to meet you!

t_ch: …呸呸呸！怎么又是那个晦气的老头子？都说了快了快了马上画完，还催！
t_en: ...Pah! That wretched old man again!? I told him, soon, soon — I'll finish it soon! Quit bugging me!

t_ch: 不过，你倒是来得正好。鄙人刚完成了这幅风景画——贝洛伯格的远景——我准备给它起名为《晨曦》！
t_en: But you've got great timing. I've just finished with this landscape — a vista overlooking Belobog — and I'm gonna call it Ray of Dawn!

t_ch: 但画是画完了…鄙人总觉得哪里好像欠了些什么——在你看来，这画作究竟如何？
t_en: It's finished, but... I can't help but feel like it's missing something, somewhere. What do you think of this painting?

t_ch: 理性分析。
t_en: Rational analysis.

t_ch: 从画面构成到色彩运用再到意向传达，你穷尽毕生所学，将自己的观点和盘托出。
t_en: From the composition to the color palette and the communication of motifs, you pull out all the stops to offer up all the insight you can give.

t_ch: <size=36>太对了！我想听的就是这个！</size>
t_en: <size=36>That's perfect! That's exactly what I wanted to hear!</size>

t_ch: 您的艺术见解真是鞭辟入里！有了您的建议，这幅画作一定要比博物馆里的地髓矿石还耀眼！
t_en: You're really insightful about art! With this kind of suggestion, this painting will be more dazzling than the geomarrow ores in the museum!

t_ch: 雷比尼按照你的建议，对画作飞快地进行了修改。
t_en: Rebini hastily makes revisions to the painting based on your feedback.

t_ch: 啊…啊啊…果然没错！这下这幅《晨曦》就是完美的作品了！
t_en: Oh... Oh my... You're right! Now this version of Ray of Dawn is the finished item!

t_ch: 谢谢你，朋友！我现在就把它送到于尔丹那个老混蛋手里——我要让他知道，我每一次拖稿都是值得的！
t_en: Thank you so much, my friend! I'm sending it in to that old man Uldan right now — I want him to know that every delay of mine was worth it!

t_ch: 雷比尼自顾自地跑走了。
t_en: Rebini runs off on his own.

t_ch: （那…稿费就归我了？）
t_en: (Then... I get to keep his fee, I suppose?)

t_ch: 吹捧一番。
t_en: Flatter the painting.

t_ch: 你将这幅完美的画作以天体运行作譬喻，充分传达了自己对大师澎湃的褒美之情——尽管你不确定大师是否知道「天体」究竟是什么。
t_en: You compare this perfect picture to celestial motion, filling every sentence with gushing praise for the great painter — despite your doubt that he even understands what "celestial motion" in fact means.

t_ch: 虽然要谢谢你的赞美…但这不是我想要的答案。
t_en: Thanks for the compliment... But that wasn't the answer I was looking for.

t_ch: 到此为止吧，我已经看出来了…你是于尔丹那个老混蛋派来催稿的——这套话术也是那个老东西教给你的，对吧？
t_en: Let's finish this. I can tell already... That old man Uldan got you to chase up his commission — he's also the one who taught you to talk like this, right?

t_ch: 既然这样，我们一手交钱、一手交货好了。他应该没忘记稿费的事吧？
t_en: In that case, I will keep things transactional. He didn't forget the fee, did he?

t_ch: 嗯，嗯…没问题，价钱没错。这幅《晨曦》就交给你了。
t_en: Mmm... All good. This is the right price. You can take this Ray of Dawn.

t_ch: 另外，托我给于尔丹带个话——我们搞艺术的，每一次拖稿都是值得的！别再成天催稿了！
t_en: By the way, send a message to Uldan for me. We artists procrastinate our art for a reason, and it cannot be rushed! So quit bugging me about it!

t_ch: 雷比尼愤愤然离开了。
t_en: Rebini storms off, enraged.

t_ch: （大师的脾气确实古怪啊……）
t_en: (The master painter really is eccentric...)

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 你的回答是不知道——你也不知道自己的此番回答会引起大师何种反应。但你坚信，真诚就是你最好的武器。
t_en: Your answer is that you don't know — and you don't know what reaction this answer will invoke within the master painter. But you firmly believe that sincerity is your best weapon.

t_ch: 虽然你没能给我想要的答案，但你的真诚打动了我！
t_en: That might not be the answer I wanted to hear, but your sincerity left me touched!

t_ch: 我早就看出来了，你其实是于尔丹那个老混蛋派来催稿的。但你却没有用他教给你的话术……
t_en: I can tell that you've been sent up by Uldan to hurry me up. But you haven't spun the same old tricks he taught you...

t_ch: 既然这样，我们一手交钱、一手交货好了。看在你真诚待人的份上，稿费我给你打八折——就当是你的跑腿费！
t_en: If that's the case, then we'll keep things transactional. You've been sincere with me, so I'll give you a twenty percent discount — keep it as your tip!

t_ch: 于尔丹那个老东西肯定没给你跑腿费吧？听我的，以后就别替他到处催稿了……
t_en: That old fossil Uldan never paid you, right? Listen. You shouldn't chase up artists for him anymore in the future...

t_ch: 嗯，嗯…没问题，价钱没错。这幅《晨曦》就交给你了。
t_en: Mmm... All good. This is the right price. You can take this Ray of Dawn.

t_ch: 另外，托我给于尔丹带个话——我们搞艺术的，每一次拖稿都是值得的！
t_en: By the way, send a message to Uldan for me. We artists procrastinate our art for a reason, and it cannot be rushed!

t_ch: 雷比尼淡然地离开了。
t_en: Rebini leaves, indifferently.

t_ch: （这位大师还挺豪爽……）
t_en: (The master paint is pretty generous...)

t_ch: 我拿到油画《晨曦》了。
t_en: I've got Ray of Dawn.

t_ch: 哦？手脚挺利索嘛。让我看看……
t_en: Hmmm? You work pretty fast. Let me have a look-see...

t_ch: 嗯…嗯嗯…《晨曦》——不错不错，这可是个好名字。
t_en: Mmmm... Mmm-hmm... Ray of Dawn — very nice. It's a great name.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。我这就去把画挂上去……
t_en: Good work, {NICKNAME}. I know just where to hang it...

t_ch: 你来了，{NICKNAME}。这次又准备收集<color=#dbc291ff>「贝洛伯格风景画」</color>了，对吗？
t_en: Hi there, {NICKNAME}. You're here to collect <color=#dbc291ff>Belobog Landscape Paintings</color> again, right?

t_ch: 让我看看，现在还缺哪些画……
t_en: Let me check which paintings are still missing...

t_ch: …好了，我知道了。这次得麻烦你跑一趟<color=#dbc291ff>大矿区</color>——不出意外的话，<color=#dbc291ff>雷比尼</color>应该就在那里。
t_en: ...Got it. This time, I'll have to task you with a trip to the <color=#dbc291ff>Great Mine</color> — unless I'm mistaken, that's where <color=#dbc291ff>Rebini</color> should be.

t_ch: 另外，这是约好的稿费，也麻烦你拿给他了。
t_en: Also, here's the fee we settled on. Please pass it on for me.

t_ch: 你瞧！穷苦的劳动者，击碎一块坚硬的岩石——
t_en: Behold! The impoverished laborer, smashing open a solid rock —

t_ch: 在他有力的锤下——自然迸出火花——！
t_en: Beneath his mighty hammer — natural sparks spurt forth —!

t_ch: 啊——肚子饿了…画不出来……
t_en: Oh — I'm so hungry... I can't paint anymore...

t_ch: 要是有<color=#dbc291ff>岩烧洞螈</color>就好了啊……
t_en: I wish I had some <color=#dbc291ff>stone-grilled olms</color>...

t_ch: 给你，岩烧洞螈。
t_en: Here you go then, some stone-grilled olms.

t_ch: 啊…这味道……
t_en: Ahhh... That smell...

t_ch: <size=36>这不是岩烧洞螈吗？！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Isn't this a stone-grilled olm!?</size>

t_ch: 雷比尼如风卷残云般消灭了一整只岩烧洞螈……
t_en: Rebini ravenously wolves down an entire stone-grilled olm...

t_ch: 啊！虽然这食材长得古怪了些，但味道果然棒极了——
t_en: Ahh! It may look weird, but the taste is just unbeatable...

t_ch: 谢谢你，不知名的好心人！有了你的帮助，我的绘画灵感开始如博物馆门口的喷泉般疯狂涌动！
t_en: Thank you, oh kind-hearted stranger! Thanks to you, I can feel my artistic inspiration surging up like the fountains outside the museum!

t_ch: 咦，总觉得你有点眼熟……
t_en: Huh, something seems really familiar about you...

t_ch: …算了，那不重要！鄙人名叫雷比尼，现在正为补充博物馆展品绞尽脑汁写生中！
t_en: ...Ah, forget it. Probably nothing! My name's Rebini, and I'm currently racking my brains about what to draw as an exhibit for the Belobog History and Culture Museum!

t_ch: 接下来，我雷比尼将为这大矿区的风景画下点睛的一笔——敬请见证！
t_en: Next up I, Rebini, will add the finishing touches to this landscape depicting the Great Mine — I beseech you to bear witness!

t_ch: 完成了！这幅画的名字就叫…《劳动者》吧！
t_en: And so, it is done! I shall name this painting... The Laborers!

t_ch: 好名字！
t_en: A great name!

t_ch: 哈哈哈！英雄所见略同！
t_en: Haha, great minds think alike.

t_ch: 所以你只是为骗一口吃的吗？！
t_en: So you're just in it for the money!?

t_ch: 错了，错了！我想你可能不太懂艺术……
t_en: No no, that's not right! I surmise that you're not an aficionado of art...

t_ch: 有时候，一幅作品无法臻于完美，缺的便正是这灵感的一笔！
t_en: Sometimes, when a piece of art cannot be perfected, all that's needed is just this stroke of inspiration!

t_ch: 接下来，我将亲自将这幅画作送往博物馆——是时候让贝城所有人民欣赏这充满力量的作品了！
t_en: I'm going to personally send this painting to the museum — it's time for all the people of Belobog to enjoy this powerful artwork!

t_ch: 回见，好心人！如果你还对这幅作品感兴趣，那就前往博物馆吧！
t_en: Until next time, kind one! If you're still interested in the painting, go check it out in the museum!

t_ch: 雷比尼大笑着离开了。
t_en: Rebini leaves, laughing.

t_ch: （看来又能挣一笔跑腿费了……）
t_en: (Looks like I can pick up another tip from this...)

t_ch: 您好，我是来催稿的。
t_en: Hello, I'm here to chase up the commission.

t_ch: 肚子好饿……
t_en: I'm sooo hungry...

t_ch: 如果吃不到<color=#dbc291ff>岩烧洞螈</color>，我来下层区究竟有什么意义？我的灵魂还算完整吗……
t_en: If I can't eat some <color=#dbc291ff>stone-grilled olms</color>, why on earth did I even come to the Underworld? Is my soul even complete in that case...

t_ch: （看来他已经快要饿疯了…想想办法吧。）
t_en: (Looks like he's about to lose his mind with hunger... Let's figure out what to do.)

t_ch: 给你钱，你自己去买吧。
t_en: Here's some money. Get some for yourself.

t_ch: 啊…世界上还是好人多啊！
t_en: Ah... Kind people still exist in this world!

t_ch: 雷比尼如疾风般从你眼前消失，复又出现在你面前——嘴角挂着尚且温热的油脂。
t_en: Rebini vanishes from your sight before reappearing — a trickle of warm grease lining the edge of his lips.

t_ch: 啊！虽然这食材长得古怪了些，但味道果然棒极了——
t_en: Ahh! It may look weird, but the taste is just unbeatable...

t_ch: 谢谢你，不知名的好心人！有了你的帮助，我的绘画灵感开始如博物馆门口的喷泉般疯狂涌动！
t_en: Thank you, oh kind-hearted stranger! Thanks to you, I can feel my artistic inspiration surging up like the fountains outside the museum!

t_ch: 我拿到油画《劳动者》了。
t_en: I've got The Laborers.

t_ch: 哦？比我想象得还要快啊。让我看看……
t_en: Really? That was much faster than anticipated. Let me have a look-see...

t_ch: 嗯…嗯嗯…《劳动者》——呵，看来这家伙又有进步了。
t_en: Mmm... Mmm-hmm... The Laborers — Ha, looks like he's improved.

t_ch: 让你受累了，{NICKNAME}。我这就去安排布置展品的事……
t_en: Thanks for the hard work, {NICKNAME}. I'm off now to arrange the exhibit...

t_ch: 欢迎回来，{NICKNAME}。这次是准备收集<color=#dbc291ff>「贝洛伯格风景画」</color>，对吗？
t_en: Welcome back, {NICKNAME}. You're here to collect <color=#dbc291ff>Belobog Landscape Paintings</color>, right?

t_ch: 让我看看，现在还缺哪些画……
t_en: Let me check which paintings are still missing...

t_ch: …好了，我知道了。这次的地点是<color=#dbc291ff>黄金歌剧院</color>——<color=#dbc291ff>雷比尼</color>应该会在那里写生。
t_en: ...Got it. This time the location is the <color=#dbc291ff>Golden Theater</color> — <color=#dbc291ff>Rebini</color> should be painting over there.

t_ch: 啊，对了，别忘了稿费——给你，就拜托你转交给他了。
t_en: Oh yes, that's right — don't forget the fee. Here you go. Pass it on to Rebini.

t_ch: 我的歌走了调，我的嗓音沙哑——
t_en: I'm out of tune and my throat is hoarse —

t_ch: 但至少——没有和声能将它伴唱——！
t_en: But at least — nobody can harmonize with me —!

t_ch: 喔，你好，这位市民！
t_en: Oh, hello there, citizen!

t_ch: 咦，怎么觉得你有点眼熟呢…也许是错觉吧。
t_en: Huh, something seems really familiar about you... I must be wrong, though.

t_ch: 不好意思，如你所见——虽然我确实是在这里写生，但我可不是画贩子。如果您想买画的话，恐怕得移步别处了。
t_en: I'm sorry. As you can see — I'm definitely painting here, but I'm no art dealer. If you want to buy something, I'm afraid you'll have to try elsewhere.

t_ch: 我就想要买你的画。
t_en: What I want to buy is one of your paintings.

t_ch: 咳咳。这可不行，我这幅作品可是贝洛伯格历史文化博物馆指名要求的稿件……
t_en: Ahem, no way. This artwork here has been commissioned by the Belobog History and Culture Museum...

t_ch: …稿件，你懂吧？支付足够的稿费，你就可以成为这幅画的主人。
t_en: ...It's a commission, get it? That means so long as you pay enough, it can be YOUR commission.

t_ch: 至于这幅画嘛——价格是<unbreak>12000</unbreak>信用点。你要是真有诚意，那就至少得再加个价……
t_en: As for the price of this one — <unbreak>12,000</unbreak> credits will do. You should pump it up a little if you're really sincere about things...

t_ch: …否则我何必撕掉和博物馆的合同呢，对吧？哈哈哈。
t_en: ...Otherwise, why would I terminate my contract with the museum, right? Haha!

t_ch: 太贵了，便宜点！
t_en: That's too much. I want it cheaper!

t_ch: 哈？你这人怎么回事？天上可不会随便掉馅饼啊！
t_en: Ha! What's wrong with you? There's no such thing as a free lunch out there!

t_ch: 你要是这么办事，那要加的价可不止原先那个数了——<unbreak>14000</unbreak>信用点，你爱要不要吧！
t_en: If you do that, then I'll have to keep putting up the price — it's <unbreak>14,000</unbreak> credits now. Take it or leave it!

t_ch: 嗯，嗯…没问题！我还以为你爱贪便宜，没想到出手倒是够大方……
t_en: Mmmm, hmmm... Fine! I took you for a miser and never thought you'd actually be so generous...

t_ch: 不管怎么样，这幅画现在是你的了。名字嘛，我暂时还没想好——但既然你是它的主人，你想起什么名字就起什么吧。
t_en: In any case, the painting is yours now. As for the name, I haven't settled on one yet — but seeing as it's yours now, you can name it whatever you like.

t_ch: 现在，我要想办法在有限的时间内再赶出一幅同样的画了…有缘再见，陌生人！
t_en: Now then, I have to figure out a way to put together another painting before my deadline... May we meet again, stranger!

t_ch: 加钱就加钱！
t_en: Fine by me!

t_ch: 不错不错，你这人够爽快！
t_en: Not bad. You really are a breath of fresh air!

t_ch: 嗯，嗯…没问题！和你做生意非常愉快，你现在就是这幅画的主人了！
t_en: Hmm, mmm... No problem! It's been a pleasure doing business with you. You are now the proud owner of this painting!

t_ch: 名字嘛，我暂时还没想好——但既然你是它的主人，这项艰巨的任务就交给你了。
t_en: As for the name, I haven't settled on one yet — but seeing as it's yours now, such a daunting task lies in your hands.

t_ch: 现在，我要想办法在有限的时间内再赶出一幅同样的画了…有缘再见，陌生人！
t_en: Now then, I have to figure out a way to put together another painting before my deadline... May we meet again, stranger!

t_ch: 其实我就是于尔丹派来的…
t_en: To be honest... Uldan sent me.

t_ch: …啧！怎么又是那个老家伙。
t_en: ...Tsk! That old man again.

t_ch: 哎，行吧行吧。反正我也画完了——把说好的稿费交给我，你就可以带着他要的画走人。
t_en: Ack, fine then. In any case, the painting's finished — hand over the payment, and you can leave with his painting.

t_ch: 嗯，嗯…没问题，价钱没错。看来那家伙还没糊涂。
t_en: Mmm... All good. This is the right price. Looks like he hasn't quite lost all his marbles just yet.

t_ch: 名字嘛，我暂时还没想好——但既然你是它的主人，这项艰巨的任务就交给你了。
t_en: As for the name, I haven't settled on one yet — but seeing as it's yours now, such a daunting task lies in your hands.

t_ch: 现在，我要为下一幅作品寻找灵感去了。有缘再见，陌生人！
t_en: Now then, I'm off to search out some inspiration for my next masterpiece. May we meet again, stranger!

t_ch: 不，我是于尔丹派来催稿的。
t_en: No, Uldan sent me to chase you up.

t_ch: …啧！怎么又是那个老家伙。
t_en: ...Tsk! That old man again.

t_ch: 哎，行吧行吧。反正我也画完了——把说好的稿费交给我，你就可以带着他要的画走人。
t_en: Ack, fine then. In any case, the painting's finished — hand over the payment, and you can leave with his painting.

t_ch: 嗯，嗯…没问题，价钱没错。看来那家伙还没糊涂。
t_en: Mmm... All good. This is the right price. Looks like he hasn't quite lost all his marbles just yet.

t_ch: 名字嘛，我暂时还没想好——但既然你是它的主人，这项艰巨的任务就交给你了。
t_en: As for the name, I haven't settled on one yet — but seeing as it's yours now, such a daunting task lies in your hands.

t_ch: 现在，我要为下一幅作品寻找灵感去了。有缘再见，陌生人！
t_en: Now then, I'm off to search out some inspiration for my next masterpiece. May we meet again, stranger!

t_ch: 我拿到绘有黄金歌剧院的画了。
t_en: I've got the painting with the Golden Theater.

t_ch: 哦？快让我看看…这小子，挺厉害的嘛。
t_en: Oh? Let me see... He's pretty darn good.

t_ch: 不过，他还没给这幅画起名字啊。{NICKNAME}，看在你为博物馆跑前跑后的份上，这幅画的冠名权就交给你吧。
t_en: But he hasn't given this painting a name yet. {NICKNAME}, you've put in the miles running after this for the museum. The honor of naming it should belong to you.

t_ch: 你觉得，该给这幅画起个什么名字好呢？
t_en: What do you think this painting should be titled?

t_ch: 《黄金歌剧院》。
t_en: Golden Theater.

t_ch: 哈哈，不错，很直白。这也正符合「巡回艺术画派」的理念——让所有人民都能理解贝洛伯格的艺术杰作。
t_en: Haha, yes — straight to the point. Certainly in line with the nomadist school of thought — an artwork that demonstrates Belobog to all kinds of people.

t_ch: …不错，不错。想必雷比尼会很喜欢这个名字，就它吧。
t_en: ...Not bad. I bet Rebini himself would love this name. So be it.

t_ch: 《戈利尔的黄金之声》。
t_en: Goliards's Golden Sound.

t_ch: 嚯，没想到啊。竟然还有人记得歌剧院首任院长的名字……
t_en: Oh, now that's unexpected. Who knew that people still remember the name of the theater's first-ever director...

t_ch: 这也是她说过的——「如果世界由冷酷的白银铸就，就用歌声的黄金将之融化」……
t_en: She did say: "If the world was forged in cold silver, then it shall be challenged by the molten gold known as music"...

t_ch: …不错，不错。我很喜欢这个名字，就它吧。
t_en: ...Not bad. I love this name. So be it.

t_ch: 《根本看不出来画了什么》。
t_en: Can't Tell What This Is.

t_ch: 这名字倒是非常…先锋。
t_en: Now that name is particularly... Avant-garde.

t_ch: 虽然我对这名字有些意见，但我毕竟还承诺了冠名权在你…所以，就它吧。
t_en: I might have my own issues with the name, but I still promised you naming rights... So be it.

t_ch: 我患有取名困难症…
t_en: I'm really indecisive about naming things...

t_ch: 哈哈，我懂你。那咱们也别折腾，干脆就叫它《黄金歌剧院》吧。
t_en: Haha, I get it. Then let's spare ourselves the trouble and just name it Golden Theater.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。这下这幅画就能用于展出了……
t_en: Good work, {NICKNAME}. Now it's ready to be displayed...

t_ch: 你来得正好，{NICKNAME}。我刚刚清点了一遍展品储藏室，意外发现了这张旧照片。
t_en: Just in the nick of time, {NICKNAME}. I've just gone over all the exhibits in the storage room, and stumbled across this old photo.

t_ch: 其中这些物件非常适合在综合展馆展出，但它们现在散落在贝洛伯格各地——凭我这把老骨头，想要独自把它们全部带回来恐怕有点困难……
t_en: These would all be great for the General Hall, but they're scattered all over Belobog now — and I'm afraid there's no way an old-timer like me can get to them all...

t_ch: 你只要在<color=#dbc291ff>大矿区</color>找到<color=#dbc291ff>操纵杆</color>，再把它带回来就好。
t_en: It would be great if you could fetch for me from <color=#dbc291ff>the Great Mine</color> this <color=#dbc291ff>operating lever</color>.

t_ch: 展品所在的大概位置，我写在了照片背面。就麻烦你跑一趟了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I've written its approximate location on the back of the photo. I have to trouble you with another trip, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （这应该就是照片上的物件了…把它带回去吧。）
t_en: (This should be the item in the photo... I'll bring it back.)

t_ch: 我找到旧照片上的展品了。
t_en: I found the exhibit item from that old photo.

t_ch: 哦？你已经找到了吗？让我看看……
t_en: Hmm? Already? Let me have a look-see...

t_ch: 嗯…没错，就是它。品相不错，简单修复一下就可以安排展出了……
t_en: Mmm... That's great. That's the one. It's still in good condition. Just a quick fix and it'll be ready for exhibition...

t_ch: 用不了多久，你就能在综合馆最显眼的位置看到它了。这次也辛苦你了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Soon enough. This'll have pride of place in the General Hall. Well done, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 你只要在<color=#dbc291ff>机械聚落</color>找到<color=#dbc291ff>能源中枢</color>，再把它带回来就好。
t_en: It would be great if you could fetch for me from <color=#dbc291ff>the Robot Settlement</color> this <color=#dbc291ff>energy hub</color>.

t_ch: 你只要在<color=#dbc291ff>边缘通路</color>找到<color=#dbc291ff>加农炮</color>，再把它带回来就好。
t_en: It would be great if you could fetch for me from <color=#dbc291ff>Backwater Pass</color> this <color=#dbc291ff>cannon</color>.

t_ch: 我来帮忙收集展品了
t_en: I'm here to help with collecting exhibits.

t_ch: 我找到藏宝图上的展品了。
t_en: I found the exhibit item from that treasure map.

t_ch: 你来得正好，{NICKNAME}。我刚刚清点了一遍展品储藏室，意外发现了这张藏宝图。
t_en: Just in the nick of time, {NICKNAME}. I've just gone over all the exhibits in the storage room, and stumbled across this treasure map.

t_ch: 展品所在的大概位置，我写在了藏宝图背面。就麻烦你跑一趟了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I've written its approximate location on the back of the treasure map. I have to trouble you with another trip, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （这应该就是藏宝图上的物件了…把它带回去吧。）
t_en: (This should be the item in the treasure map... I'll bring it back.)

t_ch: 又见面了，{NICKNAME}。你来得正巧，<color=#dbc291ff>博物馆修理间</color>刚送来一批需要修复的文物。
t_en: You again, {NICKNAME}. You're just in time: The <color=#dbc291ff>museum workshop</color> has just received a batch of artifacts that need restoring.

t_ch: 如果你有空的话，不妨搭把手吧。至于具体要怎么做，去那里直接问问<color=#dbc291ff>鲍里斯</color>就好——这是他擅长的领域。
t_en: Give them a hand if you're free. Head over and talk to <color=#dbc291ff>Boris</color> — it's his area of expertise, and he'll fill you in with exactly what needs to be done.

t_ch: 我来帮忙修复文物。
t_en: I'm here to help restore some relics.

t_ch: 哈！我正想你什么时候才到呢——来来来，我们开工！
t_en: Ha! I was just wondering when you'd be in — come on then. Let's get to it!

t_ch: 不错不错！多亏有你帮忙，这批展品提前修复完成了！
t_en: Not too bad! Thanks to you, this batch of relics has all been restored ahead of schedule!

t_ch: 我这就去找于尔丹安排展出的事——之后别忘了到展厅里欣赏欣赏自己的劳动成果啊！
t_en: I'm off to touch base with Uldan then about exhibiting them — don't forget to come back to the exhibition hall when you get the chance to check out the fruits of your labor!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了。接下来这个任务，我想一定很适合你。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're back. I have absolute confidence that you're the right person for this next job.

t_ch: 这次，我希望你能够帮忙收集<color=#dbc291ff>「自动机兵•战犬」</color>的机体数据……
t_en: This time around, I hope you can help us retrieve some data from <color=#dbc291ff>Automaton Hound</color>...

t_ch: 先前我们与银鬃铁卫技术部沟通，结果却吃了闭门羹。他们认为铁卫机兵的机体数据属于最高机密，因此不便交给博物馆制作模型。
t_en: We put in a request with the Silvermane Guards' Technology Division, but they shut us down. They said that data from Silvermane automatons is top secret, so they're unwilling to let it be an exhibit in the Museum.

t_ch: 所以，我就想到了你——听说你在下层区的人望极高，甚至连那位「史瓦罗」都愿意承认你的身份……
t_en: That's why I've thought of you — I heard you've made quite the name for yourself down in the Underworld, and even that Svarog is willing to sing your praises...

t_ch: 任务很简单：你只要带着这台记录仪器前往下层区，与<color=#dbc291ff>「自动机兵•战犬」</color>战斗一次就好。
t_en: This is a simple job: Just take this recording device down to the Underworld and get into a fight with an <color=#dbc291ff>Automaton Hound</color> — that's all.

t_ch: 下层区机兵的机体数据想来是不涉密的…那么，这件事就拜托你了。
t_en: Automaton data from the Underworld presumably is not classified... And so, I leave this task in your capable hands.

t_ch: （数据似乎已经记录下来了……）
t_en: (The data seems to have been recorded...)

t_ch: 我收集到机体数据了。
t_en: I've got the data.

t_ch: 辛苦你了，我这就把数据交给我们的模型师。
t_en: Great work. I'll pass that on to our data modeler.

t_ch: 之后，你就能在工业展馆看到我们复制的自动机兵模型了。放心，它们不会动起来的——
t_en: Later on, you'll be able to see a replica automaton over in our Industrial Hall. Don't worry. They won't move around —

t_ch: ——如果鲍里斯没有又做什么手脚的话……
t_en: — unless Boris has pulled another prank of his on us...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了。接下来这个任务，我想一定很适合你。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're back. I have absolute confidence that you're the right person for this next job.

t_ch: 这次，我希望你能够帮忙收集<color=#dbc291ff>「自动机兵•甲虫」</color>的机体数据……
t_en: This time around, I hope you can help us retrieve some data from <color=#dbc291ff>Automaton Beetle</color>...

t_ch: 任务很简单：你只要带着这台记录仪器前往下层区，与<color=#dbc291ff>「自动机兵•甲虫」</color>战斗一次就好。
t_en: This is a simple job: Just take this recording device down to the Underworld and get into a fight with an <color=#dbc291ff>Automaton Beetle</color> — that's all.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了。接下来这个任务，我想一定很适合你。

t_ch: 这次，我希望你能够帮忙收集<color=#dbc291ff>「自动机兵•蜘蛛」</color>的机体数据……
t_en: This time around, I hope you can help us retrieve some data from <color=#dbc291ff>Automaton Spider</color>...

t_ch: 任务很简单：你只要带着这台记录仪器前往下层区，与<color=#dbc291ff>「自动机兵•蜘蛛」</color>战斗一次就好。
t_en: This is a simple job: Just take this recording device down to the Underworld and get into a fight with an <color=#dbc291ff>Automaton Spider</color> — that's all.

t_ch: 下层区机兵的机体数据想来是不涉密的…那么，这件事就拜托你了。
t_en: Automaton data from the Underworld presumably is not classified... And so, I leave this task in your capable hands.

t_ch: 哦？生面孔啊。来我们机械聚落有什么事吗？
t_en: Huh? A fresh face. What have you come to our robot settlement for?

t_ch: 我想要「战犬」的机体数据。
t_en: I want some data from an automaton hound.

t_ch: 嚯，狮子大开口啊！我这自动机兵的数据可不能随便给外人。
t_en: Yeah, right. That's a pretty tall order! No way I'm handing over automaton data to just anybody.

t_ch: 不过，你要是愿意和我的大宝贝对练一番——我也不是不可以考虑。
t_en: But then again, if you're willing to spar with my big baby, then I might just be willin' to reconsider.

t_ch: 怎么样，要比划比划吗？还是说，你被我的大宝贝吓怕了？
t_en: How about it, then? A fight? Or are you scared of my baby?

t_ch: 比就比！谁怕谁？
t_en: Let's fight, then! Bring it on!

t_ch: 哈哈，不错！我的大宝贝这下又能收集到新的战斗数据了——
t_en: Haha! Good! Another chance for my sweetie to pick up some new combat data —

t_ch: 让我们开始吧！
t_en: Let's start!

t_ch: 哈哈，这下可收集到宝贵的数据了。宝贝你别急，我这就给你做升级……
t_en: Haha, now that's some precious data. Don't worry, my dear — I'll give you an upgrade...

t_ch: 原来这套机体数据就给你吧，反正我已经用不到了。
t_en: So here's the previous automaton data for you. I ain't got no use for it anymore.

t_ch: 这是你应得的——回见啦，{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}！
t_en: You earned it — seeya later, {F#sister}{M#brother}!

t_ch: （数据似乎已经记录下来了……）
t_en: (The data seems to have been recorded...)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们刚刚在整理展品的时候发现了一张旧照片。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we just stumbled across an old photo while going through the collection.

t_ch: 虽然不是很古老，但看起来也有些年份了。我猜，我们或许能在这里找到一些有价值的东西来当作展品。
t_en: It's not tremendously old, but it sure looks aged enough. I'm guessing that we might be able to find something of value from here to display.

t_ch: 看起来这些宝物都在下层区…{NICKNAME}，可能要麻烦你去找找看了。
t_en: Looks like these treasures are all in the Underworld... {NICKNAME}, can I please task you with finding them?

t_ch: 你找到了一台平平无奇的老式显示屏，虽然做工称得上精细，但布满了划痕。
t_en: You find an old-fashioned and unremarkable display screen, riddled with scratches despite its fine craftsmanship.

t_ch: 你有些疑惑这样普通的东西为什么会出现在旧照片上，或许是你的考古水平不够，看不出它隐含的价值。
t_en: You're slightly confused about why such an ordinary item thing would be captured in those old photos, but perhaps that's just because you don't have the archeological knowledge to realize its hidden value.

t_ch: 我已经找到「宝藏」了。
t_en: I've found the "treasure".

t_ch: 幸苦你了，还请让我看看…
t_en: Good job. Let me take a look-see...

t_ch: 这台座钟只是很普通的民用显示屏，没有什么稀奇的，不过是我小时候才会用的款式。
t_en: This clock is just a consumer-grade display monitor. There's nothing unusual about it. If I have to say something, it's a style I only used when I was a kid.

t_ch: 为什么它们会被画在旧照片上？
t_en: Why was this drawn on the old photos?

t_ch: 哈哈，谁知道呢，或许是恶作剧，或许是孩子们的寻宝游戏，又或许真的是对某人来说意义非凡的宝藏。
t_en: Haha, who knows! Maybe it's a prank. Maybe it's some kids on a treasure hunt. Or maybe it really is a treasure that means a lot to someone out there.

t_ch: ——就像历史里的很多事一样，我们这些后人是无法了解真相的。
t_en: Just like so many other things in history, we modern humans can scarcely perceive the truth.

t_ch: 我会把它们拿去清理修复一下，不久它们就会出现在展台上了。{NICKNAME}，谢谢你带来的「宝藏」。
t_en: I'm going to take them for cleaning and restoration. They'll be on display soon enough. {NICKNAME}, thanks for bringing me this "treasure".

t_ch: 我来帮忙收集展品了
t_en: I'm here to help with collecting exhibits.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们刚刚在整理展品的时候发现了一张藏宝图。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we just stumbled across a treasure map while going through the collection.

t_ch: 虽然不是很古老，但看起来也有些年份了。我猜，我们或许能在这里找到一些有价值的东西来当作展品。
t_en: It's not tremendously old, but it sure looks aged enough. I'm guessing that we might be able to find something of value from here to display.

t_ch: 看起来这些宝物都在下层区…{NICKNAME}，可能要麻烦你去找找看了。
t_en: Looks like these treasures are all in the Underworld... {NICKNAME}, can I please task you with finding them?

t_ch: 你找到了一组锈迹斑斑的机柜，能看出来它被废弃在这里有一段时间了。
t_en: You find a stack of rusty cabinets that have obviously been abandoned here for quite some time.

t_ch: 与上一件宝藏一样，你看不出来它的宝贵之处。如果旁人路过，只会觉得它是一件垃圾。
t_en: Like the last treasure, you cannot see the value in this. To a spectator, this would merely look like a pile of trash.

t_ch: 或许宝藏正是用这种方式掩饰着自己的价值，才能等来持有藏宝图的人来发现它吧。
t_en: Perhaps this is how the treasure hides its value until the people in possession of the treasure map come to find it.

t_ch: 我已经找到「宝藏」了。
t_en: I've found the "treasure".

t_ch: 这组机柜应该是操控矿用机械的终端，这个型号也已经被淘汰了。
t_en: These cabinets should be the terminal to control the mining gear. This model is long obsolete.

t_ch: 这样的东西能拿来当展品吗？
t_en: Can this kind of thing really be used as an exhibition?

t_ch: 当然可以，这些东西很好地记录了地下矿区的生活与工业技术——开设工业展馆的目的就是为了展示这些。
t_en: Of course! These form a good record of daily life and industrial technology used in the underground mines. The whole point of opening the Industrial Hall is to showcase exhibits such as these.

t_ch: 要不然还是扔去垃圾场吧。
t_en: Maybe we should just chuck it in the trash?

t_ch: 不必不必——我觉得完全可以把它们放在展柜里。
t_en: No need — I think it's perfectly reasonable to place them in a display cabinet.

t_ch: 展品的价值是历史赋予的，这些东西本身也承载了一段历史。虽然不够久远，但值得记录。
t_en: The value of an exhibit is endowed by history, and these things indeed all bear a certain historical weight. They might not be tremendously ancient, but they are worth recording.

t_ch: 我会把它们拿去清理修复一下，不久它们就会出现在展台上了。{NICKNAME}，谢谢你带来的「宝藏」。
t_en: I'm going to take them for cleaning and restoration. They'll be on display soon enough. {NICKNAME}, thanks for bringing me this "treasure".
